# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains a comparative snapshot of AI coding agents as of 2025, including published benchmark figures where available, explicit gaps where vendors have not released official numbers, and short product capability descriptions tied to each tool.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-013
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: metric
citation: Benched.ai, Top Coding Agents (2025)
source_url: https://benched.ai/guides/top-coding-agents-2025
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
OpenAI Codex CLI is described as having an internal SWE-task benchmark, but the source says no benchmark numbers were published.

## Source Excerpt

"Internal SWE-task benchmark mentioned but numbers not published"

## Note

The source names a benchmark but does not provide the underlying result values.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-013
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: method
citation: Benched.ai, Top Coding Agents (2025)
source_url: https://benched.ai/guides/top-coding-agents-2025
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
OpenAI Codex CLI is described as a local terminal CLI that reads, edits, and runs code locally with user-approved shell commands.

## Source Excerpt

"Local terminal CLI; open-source on GitHub" and "Reads/edits/runs code locally with user-approved shell commands"

## Note

This is a packaging and operating-mode claim, not a reported performance result.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-013
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: metric
citation: Benched.ai, Top Coding Agents (2025)
source_url: https://benched.ai/guides/top-coding-agents-2025
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Claude Code is reported with SWE-bench Verified at 72.5% for Opus 4 and Terminal-bench at 43.2%.

## Source Excerpt

"SWE-bench Verified 72.5 % (Opus 4), Terminal-bench 43.2 %"

## Note

These figures are benchmark-specific and do not by themselves establish performance on other coding tasks.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-013
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: metric
citation: Benched.ai, Top Coding Agents (2025)
source_url: https://benched.ai/guides/top-coding-agents-2025
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Claude Code is also reported with Sonnet 3.5 at 49% SWE-bench Verified and 93.7% HumanEval.

## Source Excerpt

"Sonnet 3.5: 49 % SWE-bench Verified & 93.7 % HumanEval"

## Note

The source reports these as separate benchmark scores for the Sonnet 3.5 model line.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-013
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: metric
citation: Benched.ai, Top Coding Agents (2025)
source_url: https://benched.ai/guides/top-coding-agents-2025
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Devin is reported at 13.86% end-to-end on a 25% SWE-bench Verified subset, with a prior state of the art listed as 1.96%.

## Source Excerpt

"SWE-bench Verified 13.86 % end-to-end on 25 % subset – prior SOTA 1.96 %"

## Note

The reported Devin figure is explicitly limited to a 25% subset rather than the full benchmark.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-013
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: method
citation: Benched.ai, Top Coding Agents (2025)
source_url: https://benched.ai/guides/top-coding-agents-2025
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Devin is described as planning and executing thousands of shell, editor, and browser steps and producing pull requests.

## Source Excerpt

"Plans & executes thousands of shell/editor/browser steps; produces PRs, learns new tools autonomously"

## Note

This is a capability description from the comparison table rather than a quantified benchmark.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-013
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: claim-support
citation: Benched.ai, Top Coding Agents (2025)
source_url: https://benched.ai/guides/top-coding-agents-2025
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Replit Agent + Code Repair 7B is described as outperforming GPT-4-Turbo and Claude Opus on Replit-repair and DebugBench, but the source says exact percentages were not published.

## Source Excerpt

"Outperforms GPT-4-Turbo, Claude Opus on Replit-repair & DebugBench; exact % not published, charts show top rank"

## Note

The source presents a relative superiority claim while also stating that exact numeric results were not published.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-013
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: metric
citation: Benched.ai, Top Coding Agents (2025)
source_url: https://benched.ai/guides/top-coding-agents-2025
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
GitHub Copilot is reported with a Microsoft WorkLab study saying users were 29% faster, 70% felt more productive, and 85% reached a first draft faster.

## Source Excerpt

"Microsoft WorkLab study: users 29 % faster, 70 % feel more productive, 85 % reach first draft faster"

## Note

These are productivity-study results rather than classical coding benchmark scores.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-013
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: metric
citation: Benched.ai, Top Coding Agents (2025)
source_url: https://benched.ai/guides/top-coding-agents-2025
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Supermaven is reported with 250 ms latency versus Copilot at 783 ms and a 300k-token context window.

## Source Excerpt

"Latency: 250 ms vs Copilot 783 ms; 300 k-token context window"

## Note

The source pairs a latency comparison with a stated context-window size.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-013
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: metric
citation: Benched.ai, Top Coding Agents (2025)
source_url: https://benched.ai/guides/top-coding-agents-2025
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Windsurf Cascade is reported with telemetry claiming 90% of user code is auto-generated and 57 million lines are produced per day.

## Source Excerpt

"Telemetry: 90 % of user code auto-generated; 57 M lines per day"

## Note

These are usage telemetry figures, not benchmark results from a standardized coding test.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-013
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: metric
citation: Benched.ai, Top Coding Agents (2025)
source_url: https://benched.ai/guides/top-coding-agents-2025
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Vercel v0 is reported with internal UI-task error-free generation scores of 93.87% for v0-1.5-md and 89.80% for v0-1.5-lg, compared with Claude 4 Opus at 78.43%.

## Source Excerpt

"Internal UI-task eval: error-free generation 93.87 % (v0-1.5-md), 89.80 % (lg) – beats Claude 4 Opus 78.43 %"

## Note

The source specifies that these are internal UI-task evaluation numbers rather than a general coding benchmark.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-21-013
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: claim-support
citation: Benched.ai, Top Coding Agents (2025)
source_url: https://benched.ai/guides/top-coding-agents-2025
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The source says coding-agent benchmarks measure different task types and should be matched to the intended workload before declaring a winner.

## Source Excerpt

"Benchmarks such as SWE-bench Verified, HumanEval, Terminal-bench, and proprietary 'error-free UI' tests measure different aspects of coding agents (bug-fixing across repos, Python function correctness, multi-step terminal tasks, and HTML/CSS/React fidelity, respectively). Match the benchmark to your workload before declaring a winner."

## Note

This statement limits direct cross-benchmark comparisons by stating that the reported tests evaluate different behaviors.
---

## Source Excerpt

"Benchmarks such as SWE-bench Verified, HumanEval, Terminal-bench, and proprietary 'error-free UI' tests measure different aspects of coding agents (bug-fixing across repos, Python function correctness, multi-step terminal tasks, and HTML/CSS/React fidelity, respectively). Match the benchmark to your workload before declaring a winner."

## Note

This statement limits direct cross-benchmark comparisons by stating that the reported tests evaluate different behaviors.
